cases.passage: The Indian government has been implementing various policies to boost the country's economic growth. One such policy is the 'Make in India' initiative, which aims to increase domestic production and reduce dependence on imports. As a result, the manufacturing sector has seen a significant increase in production, leading to higher output and a rise in national income. However, this growth has also led to an increase in income inequality, with the wealthy benefiting more from the growth than the poor. The government is now considering ways to redistribute the wealth and ensure that the benefits of growth are shared more equitably among all sections of society.

questions.q: What is the primary objective of the 'Make in India' initiative?
opts: (A) To reduce income inequality
(B) To increase domestic production and reduce imports
(C) To boost agricultural production
(D) To increase government expenditure
questions.explanation: The 'Make in India' initiative aims to increase domestic production and reduce dependence on imports.

questions.q: How has the growth in the manufacturing sector affected national income?
opts: (A) It has decreased national income
(B) It has kept national income constant
(C) It has increased national income
(D) It has no effect on national income
questions.explanation: The growth in the manufacturing sector has led to higher output and a rise in national income.

questions.q: What is the consequence of the growth in the manufacturing sector on income inequality?
opts: (A) It has reduced income inequality
(B) It has kept income inequality constant
(C) It has increased income inequality
(D) It has no effect on income inequality
questions.explanation: The growth in the manufacturing sector has led to an increase in income inequality, with the wealthy benefiting more from the growth than the poor.

questions.q: What is the government considering to address the issue of income inequality?
opts: (A) Increasing government expenditure
(B) Redistributing wealth to ensure benefits are shared equitably
(C) Reducing domestic production
(D) Increasing imports
questions.explanation: The government is considering ways to redistribute the wealth and ensure that the benefits of growth are shared more equitably among all sections of society.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the growth in the manufacturing sector and national income?
opts: (A) There is no relationship between the growth in the manufacturing sector and national income
(B) The growth in the manufacturing sector is negatively related to national income
(C) The growth in the manufacturing sector is positively related to national income
(D) The growth in the manufacturing sector is unrelated to national income
questions.explanation: The growth in the manufacturing sector has led to higher output and a rise in national income.

cases.passage: The Indian government has been implementing a policy of providing subsidies to farmers to encourage agricultural production. However, this policy has resulted in a significant increase in the price of food items, making them unaffordable for many consumers. As a result, the government is now considering ways to reduce the subsidies and make food items more affordable for the poor. On the other hand, the increase in agricultural production has led to an increase in national income, as the farmers are now earning more from their crops.

questions.q: What is the consequence of providing subsidies to farmers on the price of food items?
opts: (A) The price of food items has decreased
(B) The price of food items has increased
(C) The price of food items has remained constant
(D) The price of food items has decreased for some but increased for others
questions.explanation: The policy of providing subsidies to farmers has resulted in a significant increase in the price of food items.

questions.q: How has the increase in agricultural production affected national income?
opts: (A) It has decreased national income
(B) It has kept national income constant
(C) It has increased national income
(D) It has no effect on national income
questions.explanation: The increase in agricultural production has led to an increase in national income, as the farmers are now earning more from their crops.

questions.q: What is the government considering to address the issue of unaffordable food items?
opts: (A) Increasing subsidies to farmers
(B) Reducing subsidies to farmers to make food items more affordable
(C) Increasing taxes on food items
(D) Reducing production in agriculture
questions.explanation: The government is considering ways to reduce the subsidies and make food items more affordable for the poor.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the increase in agricultural production and national income?
opts: (A) There is no relationship between the increase in agricultural production and national income
(B) The increase in agricultural production is negatively related to national income
(C) The increase in agricultural production is positively related to national income
(D) The increase in agricultural production is unrelated to national income
questions.explanation: The increase in agricultural production has led to an increase in national income, as the farmers are now earning more from their crops.

questions.q: What is the effect of the increase in agricultural production on farmers?
opts: (A) It has decreased their earnings
(B) It has kept their earnings constant
(C) It has increased their earnings
(D) It has no effect on their earnings
questions.explanation: The increase in agricultural production has led to an increase in national income, as the farmers are now earning more from their crops.
